titleStmt.title: SII 33.39: original edition by K. Munirathnam and T. S. Ravishankar
No. 39. (A.R. No. 145 of 1946.) Santakōvūru, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.

summary.p: Pillar set up in front of the Āñjanēya temple.
This is dated Śaka 1454, Nandana, Māgha śu 11, Wednesday corresponding to 1533 A.D., February 5, Wednesday °75.

Records a gift of toll-income, marriage tax (Peṇḍlisuṅkamu) and land to the gods Chennakēśvara and Hanumān of Kōvūru in Poṭladoriti-sima for celebrating the festivals on the occasion of Bhīshma-ēkādaśi, for the merit of the king by Rāmānāyaka, son of Pāpināyaka, grandson of Rāmānāyaka and great grandson of Aubhaḷa-nāyaka, who held the village as the amara fief. The village was under the administration of Salkarāja Peda-Tirumalarāja, who received the Chennuri-poṭladurti-sīma from Achyutarāya as his fiefdom.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
śubhamastu śrīmate
śrīrāmānujanamaḥ
svastiśrī jayābhyu
daya śālivāha
na śakavaruṣaṁbu
lu 1454 A
gunēṭi . naṁda
na saṁvvatsara mā
gha śu 11 bu śrī maṁnma
hānāyaṁkācāryya
layina tarugoṁḍa Avubha
ḷārytaniṁgāri prapautṛlaina
rāmānāyaniṁgāri pau
tṛlu pāpināyaniṁgāri
putṛṁḍu rāmānāyani
gāru kovūriceṁnnakēśvarā
nikiṁnni hanumaṁtadēvu
kiṁnni daṁḍḍaṁ samarpiṁcciI
ccina dharmaśāsanamu śrī A
cyutadēvamahārāya
lu salkarāju pedatiruma
lrājuṁgāriki kṛpaśeya
nu Avadhariṁccina ce
rnūrū poṭla do
ritisīmalō
nu māku Ama
rānaku pāliṁci
na kovūri peṁḍlisu
ṁkhamuṁnnu pedace
ruvukiṁdda tōṁṭa
ṁstaḷānanu maḍi 81 tūme
ṁḍa peḍatāḷa tūrpucē
nu 82 yiddumun vubha
yamunnu 83

ceṁnakeśvaru
ḍikinni hanumaṁtade
vakinni parvatithulamahō
tsavālaku yībhīṣmayā
kādaśi puṇyakālamaṁdu
śrī Accutadevamahārā
yalaku puṇyaṁgānu Ā
caṁdrārkkastāIgā
nu dhāravōsi Istimi
gāna yīdhaṁrmānaku ya
vvaru dappinānu gaṁgakaru
ta gobrāṁhmaṇa vadhiṁci
na pāpānaṁ bōvuvāru (ta)
ma tallitaṁḍrini vāraṇāsilo
vadhiṁccina pāpānabōyina
vāru
Digital edition of SII 33.39 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
